title: Parameterized class typedef as interface type parameter fails
body:
Hi,

PR #6834 added support for using a parameterized class typedef as a module type parameter (fixing #6754). However, the same pattern doesn't work when the typedef is used as an interface type parameter instead.

**Example:**

```systemverilog
class B #(parameter int WIDTH=32);
    typedef struct packed { logic [WIDTH-1:0] data; } pld_t;
endclass

interface A #(parameter type PLD_T = logic);
    PLD_T pld;
endinterface

module t;
    // Using the typedef as an interface type parameter fails
    A #(B#(64)::pld_t) w_A_Bpld ();
endmodule
```

**Output:**

```
%Error: test.sv:11:26: Can't find typedef/interface: 'pld_t'
                      : ... note: In instance 't'
   11 |     A #(B#(64)::pld_t) w_A_Bpld ();
      |                          ^~
```

If I change `A` to a module instead of an interface, it works correctly (after PR #6834).

**Analysis:**

Looking at the debug output, it seems that interfaces are processed before modules in V3Param's work queue. When `cellPinCleanup()` runs for the interface cell, the `resolveDotToTypedef()` function (added in PR #6834) is called, but the parameterized class hasn't been specialized yet at that point. The DOT resolution is deferred (`deferring until post-V3Param`) but for interfaces the deferred resolution doesn't seem to trigger the class specialization path.

I'm working on a fix that ensures `classRefDeparam()` gets called to specialize the class before resolving the typedef, similar to how it works for modules.

**Command:**

```bash
verilator --cc test.sv
```

---

Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-62-ga05cbd838
